name: acacia confusa
mechanism: Likely mediated in part by flavonoid/polyphenol antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity
contraindications: avoid with unstable mental health conditions
safetyNotes: Potentially toxic; avoid unsupervised use; Avoid with unstable mental health conditions; May interact with maois

description: A perennial tree native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific, traditionally used in spiritual practices. Its root bark contains alkaloids used in entheogenic brews.
